``` ::: ::: :::
:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Release Eligibility Determined?

In general, offenders are eligible for parole upon completion of the minimum term less good time. If an offender has been sentenced to consecutive terms, the minimum term may be calculated by adding together all minimum terms. Parole may be granted only to individuals who have been committed to the Department of Corrections. Thus, all misdemeanants and felony offenders serving a term of imprisonment of under one year in the county jail do not qualify for parole release.


People who were Eligible for Parole

In 2020, White, non-Hispanic people constituted the most number of people eligible for parole but still in prison, accounting for 46% (52 people) of the parole-eligible prison population.



In 2020, there were people who were incarcerated for a parole revocation and were eligible for parole but not yet released from prison.

``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} :::
## Prison Population
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population

In 2020, there were 114 people who were eligible for parole but not released from prison, constituting 2% of the parole-eligible prison population.




``` ::: :::

## Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Most Serious Sentenced Offense

In 2020, there were 31 people who were parole eligible but still in prison for non-violent offenses, accounting for 27% of the parole-eligible prison population.

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Overview ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Racial and Ethnic Disparities

Black people were X times less/more likely than White people to be released on or before their parole eligibility year.

Hispanic people were X times less/more likely than White people to be released on or before their parole eligibility year.


For every White person who is released after their parole eligibility year, X Black people are released after their parole eligibility year.

For every White person who is released after their parole eligibility year, X Hispanic people are released after their parole eligibility year.
